behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from recommendation results regardless of relevance score.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the handoff between catalog-availability filtering and ranking/output assembly. A unit test of only the filter or only the ranker can miss reintroduction of out-of-stock items later in the pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run the wired recommendation pipeline with a fixture catalog containing high-scoring out-of-stock items and lower-scoring in-stock items; assert no out-of-stock product appears in final ranked pages.

behaviors.description: User preference filter excludes categories explicitly blocked in user settings.
behaviors.justification: The risk is in module interaction: settings retrieval + preference filter + candidate assembly. Pure function tests can pass while wiring bugs still leak blocked categories.
behaviors.test_description: With a user profile that blocks category X, run recommendation generation against a mixed-category catalog and assert no X-category products are returned.

behaviors.description: Products matching recent browsing patterns are ranked higher than products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: This depends on feature extraction from browsing events feeding the ranker correctly. The defect often appears at the event-feature-ranker boundary, not only inside a single scoring function.
behaviors.test_description: Seed browsing history for category/topic A, create candidates with/without A signal, run full ranking path, and assert A-matching products outrank otherwise comparable no-signal products.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns the correct page with stable ordering; requesting page 2 twice yields identical results when data is unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Stable paging correctness requires ranking order + pagination logic + tie-break behavior working together. Unit tests on paginator alone miss unstable upstream ordering.
behaviors.test_description: Generate recommendations for a fixed dataset/state, request page 2 twice, and assert identical ordered IDs and page boundaries across both calls.

behaviors.description: Algorithm surfaces at least one product from an unseen category (serendipity) to avoid filter bubbles.
behaviors.justification: Serendipity is a cross-signal ranking policy (history + exploration rule). Failures usually come from policy composition, candidate generation, and final ranking interplay.
behaviors.test_description: For a user with history only in categories A/B and a catalog including C, run recommendation generation and assert at least one top-N result belongs to a previously unseen category C.

behaviors.description: New user with no purchase/browsing history gets popularity-based recommendations instead of empty results.
behaviors.justification: Cold-start behavior depends on profile-state detection plus fallback strategy selection plus popularity source wiring. A unit test of fallback logic alone can miss empty-output regressions.
behaviors.test_description: Create a user with no history, run recommendation generation, assert non-empty results and that ordering follows configured popularity signal.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records correct product ID, list position, and timestamp for each clicked recommendation.
behaviors.justification: The real risk is event payload assembly and persistence wiring (UI/API payload -> tracking module -> storage). Mocking storage can hide schema/serialization mistakes.
behaviors.test_description: Serve a recommendation list, simulate a click on a known item/position, and assert persisted tracking record contains exact product_id, displayed position, and valid event timestamp.

behaviors.description: Recommendation generation stays under 200ms for a 100,000-product catalog.
behaviors.justification: This is a performance SLO sensitive to real process/runtime behavior (CPU, memory, query execution, serialization). Lower-level mocked tests can systematically miss latency regressions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end recommendation requests in a production-like runtime with a 100k-product dataset, measure p95/p99 latency, and assert the defined threshold (200ms) is met.

behaviors.description: When a recommended product is purchased, subsequent recommendations deprioritize that product.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears across state update and retrieval boundaries: purchase event ingestion, user-state update, and next ranking request. Single-function tests miss stale-state or propagation bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Request recommendations, mark one recommended product as purchased, issue a follow-up recommendation request, and assert that product’s rank drops (or is removed per policy).

behaviors.description: Recommendations are deterministic for identical user state; two requests with no intervening activity return the same ranked list.
behaviors.justification: Determinism depends on pipeline-wide behavior (feature reads, sorting tie-breakers, randomness controls, caching), not just one pure function.
behaviors.test_description: Freeze user/catalog state, call recommendation endpoint twice, and assert identical ordered product IDs and scores (or stable tie-break order) across both responses.
